reasoning: The question asks if 'Violence against civilians' event counts in PNG for the 30 days prior to March 22, 2026 will exceed 10x the reference value (1 + average 30-day count over the preceding 360 days). The last known reference value was 1.750, meaning the historical average is ~0.75 events per 30 days (or ~9 per year). To answer YES, there would need to be at least 18 individual ACLED-coded 'Violence against civilians' (VAC) events in the single 30-day period from Feb 20 to Mar 21, 2026. Achieving 18 VAC events in 30 days would represent roughly double the entire previous 360 days' tally occurring within just one month. Exhaustive searches for violence, attacks, or massacres in PNG during February and March 2026 yielded no evidence of such an explosive and unprecedented surge in attacks on unarmed clear civilians. Furthermore, ACLED classifies fighting between armed tribal groups as 'Battles', not VAC. Given this extremely high threshold and the lack of news reports indicating a continuous wave of localized attacks that could generate 18 separate VAC events, the probability of hitting this 10x multiplier is at the absolute floor.
